Title: Data Scientist til arbejdet med kunstig intelligens i Digitaliseringsstyrelsen
Place: Copenhagen Municipality, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Vil du være med til at forme, hvordan kunstig intelligens og sprogmodeller anvendes i samfundet? I Digitaliseringsstyrelsen driver vi den digitale udvikling af Danmark i tæt samarbejde med offentlige aktører, private organisationer og forskningsinstitutioner. Vi mangler dig, der vil være vores tekniske ekspert indenfor kunstig intelligens, herunder sprogteknologi og sprogmodeller.





Du kommer til at indgå i arbejdet med flere af styrelsens AI-indsatser og får en central rolle i at omsætte idéer til konkrete løsninger og understøtte de politiske ambitioner på området. Derudover bliver du en del af en fagligt stærk arbejdsplads i hjertet af København.



Kontor for AI, analyse og inklusion



Kontor for AI, analyse og inklusion udvikler politiske initiativer inden for digitalisering baseret på statistiske og kvalitative analyser i samarbejde med både offentlige og private aktører. Vi arbejder med AI, sprogteknologi og varetager også indsatsen for digital inklusion og erhvervsudvikling.





Kontoret består af 34 kollegaer med forskellige faglige baggrunde, fra etnologer til statskundskabere. Vi fokuserer på at understøtte digitaliseringen i det offentlige og fremme erhvervslivets digitale udvikling.





Du kommer til at være en del af et AI-team med ni andre kollegaer, som sætter pris på godt humør og lykkes med processer i fællesskab.



Dine arbejdsopgaver



Du bliver kontorets tekniske ekspert indenfor kunstig intelligens, herunder sprogteknologi og sprogmodeller, og du får en central rolle i at omsætte idéer til konkrete løsninger. Samtidig bliver du en vigtig sparringspartner i det tværfaglige samarbejde i Digitaliseringsstyrelsen, hvor de tekniske muligheder skal balanceres med strategiske hensyn.





Du vil blandt andet skulle bidrage til arbejdet med regeringens strategiske indsats for kunstig intelligens, hvor målsætningen er at tilgængeliggøre åbne danske tekstdata fra Rigsarkivet, Det Kgl. Bibliotek og Folketinget. Du vil ligeledes skulle indgå i samarbejde med både offentlige aktører, private organisationer og forskningsinstitutioner om at udvikle og tilgængeliggøre nye danske sprogressourcer.





Dine opgaver vil variere og kan blandt andet omfatte:






Rådgivning om tekniske problemstillinger på tværs af styrelsens AI-indsatser samt om anvendelse og evaluering af AI, for eksempel sprogmodeller og sprogteknologi


Medvirke til at sætte retning for styrelsens arbejde med AI og bidrage til udvikling af nye initiativer


Understøtte arbejdet med styrelses interne AI-værktøjer for eksempel gennem benchmark af OCR-værktøjer og valg af llm


Bidrage til arbejdet med at udvikle og tilgængeliggøre nye danske sprogressourcer. Her vil du blive inddraget i hele data pipelinen fra dataindsamling og -bearbejdning til modellering, evaluering og formidling


Afsøge mulighederne for at anvende nye metoder som federated learning eller syntetiske data til at forbedre og evaluere sprogmodeller


Bidrage til at styrelsens arbejde følger moderne tekniske standarder og best practices inden for datadeling og evaluering af sprogmodeller


Bidrage til styrelsens arbejde med tekniske standarder relateret til AI-forordningen herunder deltage i udviklingen af nye europæiske standarder




Om dig



Lyder det som noget for dig? Vi leder efter dig, der har en relevant lang videregående uddannelse inden for eksempelvis data science, social data science, maskinlæring eller en anden uddannelse, hvor du har opbygget stærke tekniske kompetencer.





Stillingen kræver, at du både kan arbejde med data og udvikling og samtidig bidrage til at sætte retning for, hvordan vi bedst muligt understøtter samfundets anvendelse af kunstig intelligens.





Du kan være nyuddannet eller have flere års erfaring. Det vigtigste er, at du trives i en rolle, hvor du er den primære tekniske profil i et team, der overvejende består af generalister.





Vi forestiller os, at du:






Kan formidle komplekse tekniske problemstillinger på en forståelig måde for ikke-tekniske målgrupper og beslutningstagere


Kan arbejde selvstændigt og tage teknisk ejerskab, også når opgaverne er åbne og eksplorative


Har erfaring med at arbejde med data og udvikle prototyper, og motiveres af at omsætte idéer til konkrete løsninger


Trives med at være bindeleddet mellem hvad der er teknisk muligt og strategisk efterspurgt




Digitaliseringsstyrelsen som arbejdsplads



Du kender os måske for MitID, Digital Post og borger.dk. I Digitaliseringsstyrelsen står vi i spidsen for en ambitiøs digital udvikling af Danmark, der skaber værdi for borgere, virksomheder og offentlige organisationer og bidrager med løsninger på samfundets store udfordringer. Vi er en styrelse med cirka 600 medarbejdere under Digitaliseringsministeriet.





Vi tager et fælles ansvar for at være en god arbejdsplads, hvor det er sjovt at gå på arbejde, og hvor der er plads til at udvikle sig. Vi bruger hinandens forskellige kompetencer, og tror på, at vi kommer bedre og hurtigere i mål, når vi samarbejder, videndeler og koordinerer. Vi tilbyder spændende faglige netværk og mange sociale arrangementer på tværs af huset.





Til daglig mødes vi som udgangspunkt på vores kontor i København tæt ved Kongens Nytorv, men du kan løbende aftale hjemmearbejde med din chef.





Du kan læse mere om, hvordan det er at arbejde hos os på digst.dk.



Ansættelsesvilkår



Der er to ledige fuldtidsstillinger på hver gennemsnitligt 37 timer om ugen, og vi regner med at byde vores nye kollegaer velkommen den 1. august 2026.





Digitaliseringsstyrelsen er placeret på Landgreven 4, 1301 København.





Du ansættes efter overenskomst for akademikere i staten og vil blive indplaceret som fuldmægtig eller konsulent med mulighed for at forhandle tillæg på baggrund af dine kvalifikationer.





Ansættelsesområdet er Digitaliseringsstyrelsen under Digitaliseringsministeriet.



Flere oplysninger



Hvis du vil vide mere om stillingen, er du velkommen til at kontakte din kommende personaleleder, Souschef Martin Jensen Buch, på telefon 2160 2613 eller e-mail majebu@digst.dk. Send Martin en SMS, så ringer han dig op.



Ansøgning



Vi ser frem til at modtage din ansøgning med CV, eksamenspapirer og eventuelle bilag via knappen ”Søg stillingen” senest den 26. maj 2026.





Vi forventer at holde samtaler i uge 23 og 24.





Vi ser mangfoldighed som en styrke og opfordrer alle interesserede kandidater uanset personlig baggrund til at søge. Vi ser frem til at læse din ansøgning.

Title: Applied AI Engineer
Description:
Tech-first. Ego-free. User-obsessed.




Over one million Danes use Skatteguiden to understand and optimise their taxes. We automate tax tracking, surface deductions people didn't know they had, and give users the insights they need to make better financial decisions — all directly connected to the Danish tax authority.




We're an independent company of around 50 people based in Copenhagen, with a 4.7-star rating and a simple mission: make financial insight accessible to everyone, not just those who can afford an accountant.




We have an ambitious multi-year vision for where we take the product next. This role helps build the conversational and agentic layer that makes it credible.







About the role




You'll work primarily on the LLM-powered parts of Skatteguiden. Today, that's a mix of customer-facing and internal products: RAG-based assistants and agentic systems, running on Azure infrastructure.




Over time, the agentic side of this stack will grow significantly, and the infrastructure needed to get there safely is part of the job.




This is a role about building agentic systems the right way — with quality, evaluations, and guardrails as first-class concerns. Alongside your team, you'll regularly partner with our Head of Security & Privacy and our Legal/Compliance team.




You'll join the AI Products team, working closely with our existing Data Scientist, our AI Engineer, and our incoming Senior ML Engineer. While we each have our specialisations and focus, we enjoy collaborating across our roles as our team is small and dynamic.







What you'll do




- Build on our existing LLM products. Ship improvements to quality, latency, cost, and safety. These are production systems used every day by users and internal teams.

- Build out the evaluation infrastructure. Offline evals, online validation, regression tracking, LLM-as-a-judge pipelines where they make sense. Evals aren't an afterthought at Skatteguiden; they drive our product decisions.

- Help define SLOs. Latency, quality, cost, and safety. Formal SLOs for production LLM systems are part of the bread and butter. You'll help define them.

- Contribute to the agentic trust infrastructure. Human-in-the-loop review flows, explicit consent, reversibility, and audit trails. As the product moves towards further maturity, this is what separates a product users trust from one they don't.

- Partner on red teaming and compliance. Work with Security & Privacy on adversarial testing and with Legal on regulatory fit.

- Stay on top of the model landscape. Azure today, but the landscape moves fast. You'll help us stay current and avoid lock-in.







Who we're looking for




You're an engineer who has built LLM systems and has opinions about how to do it well. You think evals are a first-class concern. You design for challenges like hallucinations and prompt injection. You know agentic systems are exciting, but don't ship themselves.




You've shipped LLM systems that real users touched, you take evaluation seriously as an engineering discipline, and you have the instinct to know when something is working and when it isn't. If that's you, we'd like to hear from you.




This role spans more than just LLM work. Evaluation infrastructure, integration, guardrails, and the critical engineering around production AI systems are a big part of the job.







Must-have




- Working experience building LLM-powered applications in production. Not a weekend demo — something real users have touched.

- Hands-on grasp of RAG, tool use, and at least one orchestration framework (LangGraph, OpenAI Agents SDK, Claude Agent SDK, or equivalent).

- Experience with LLM evaluation; offline evals, online validation, or regression tracking. You treat quality as an engineering discipline, not a gut check.

- Solid software engineering foundations. Python, clean code, testing, CI/CD.

- Security and privacy awareness. You understand why guardrails matter for a product that handles financial data.

- Good judgment under ambiguity. LLM work is full of unclear trade-offs; you can reason about them and pick a direction.

- Genuine enthusiasm for AI. You use AI tools daily in your own work, and you're always looking for new ways to apply them.







Nice-to-have




- Experience with production agentic systems — tool use, planning, human-in-the-loop review flows.

- Experience with Azure OpenAI and Azure AI Search.

- Experience with Agent-to-Agent (A2A) or similar protocols

- Prior work in fintech, healthtech, or another regulated domain.

- Familiarity with LLM observability tooling.

- Danish language is useful but not required. The team works in English.







Why join us




- A vision with real ambition. From conversational guidance today to an AI Companion that helps users plan and eventually act on their behalf. You'll help build the layer that makes it safe and credible.

- Quality and evals as a first-class discipline. Not a checkbox. Not a sprint goal. A permanent part of the job, with real backing from leadership.

- Room to grow. Mid-level today, but the scope of the work and the team's growth mean you'll have real room to step up. This is a good role to grow in seniority.

- The product matters. Over one million Danes use Skatteguiden. Your work keeps it trustworthy.

- Solid company. Independent, profitable, growing. No VC pressure, no bloated roadmap. Just a clear mission and a team that cares.

- A company that's serious about AI. We use AI across the business, and we're only going deeper. Being a small, focused team means we can move fast on this — no lengthy approval chains, no slow rollouts.

- Good people. We take the work seriously without taking ourselves too seriously. The culture is professional, informal, and genuinely collaborative.







Practical details




Location: Copenhagen. We're an office-first company, and it's a deliberate choice. We're moving fast, we have big ambitions, and we genuinely believe that being physically together is one of the things that makes that possible. Things move quicker when you can turn your chair and talk to someone.




We're a five-minute walk from Rådhuspladsen, stocked with everything from fruit to fizzy drinks to afternoon snacks, and lunch is at Claus Meyer's canteen just across the street — proper food, not an afterthought. If you're putting in a long day, dinner is covered too.




Start: As soon as possible.




Salary: Competitive and based on your experience.







Our lean hiring process




1. Screening call with our recruiter (30 min) ONLINE

2. Technical interview with the Head of AI Products and an AI engineer (120 min) ONSITE

3. Case / technical deep-dive with the AI Products team (60 min) ONSITE

4. Final conversation with the CTO and CEO (30 min) ONSITE

- Good people. We take the work seriously without taking ourselves too seriously. The culture is professional, informal, and genuinely collaborative.



Practical details


Location: Copenhagen. We're an office-first company, and it's a deliberate choice. We're moving fast, we have big ambitions, and we genuinely believe that being physically together is one of the things that makes that possible. Things move quicker when you can turn your chair and talk to someone.


We're a five-minute walk from Rådhuspladsen, stocked with everything from fruit to fizzy drinks to afternoon snacks, and lunch is at Claus Meyer's canteen just across the street — proper food, not an afterthought. If you're putting in a long day, dinner is covered too.


Start: As soon as possible.


Salary: Competitive and based on your experience.



Our lean hiring process


1. Screening call with our recruiter (30 min) ONLINE

2. Technical interview with the Head of AI Products and an AI engineer (120 min) ONSITE

3. Case / technical deep-dive with the AI Products team (60 min) ONSITE

4. Final conversation with the CTO and CEO (30 min) ONSITE

Title: Data Science Specialist - Cross-Trial Evidence & HTA
Place: Gladsaxe, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Cross-Trial Evidence & HTA Operations

Søborg or Aalborg (DK), Onsite, or United Kingdom or Spain, Virtual

For people living with serious chronic diseases, an approved medicine is only as good as the access they have to it. In market after market, HTA bodies, reimbursement authorities and payers must be convinced by rigorous, credible evidence before that access becomes real. This is what we drive in Cross-Trial Evidence & HTA, and we are looking for someone to help us do it better.

Your new role

You will drive technical execution and innovation across our obesity, diabetes, and rare disease portfolio, at the intersection where data science and statistics meet evidence synthesis and health technology assessment.

Engaging across multiple assets and the full development lifecycle will give you a unique scope: you will identify patterns others miss, build infrastructure that scales portfolio-wide, and see how your analytical solutions directly shape evidence strategies and market access outcomes.

We are committed to embedding AI-augmentation deeply into our workflows, and this role sits at the heart of that effort: designing, implementing, and validating approaches that preserve scientific rigor while fundamentally changing what a team of our size can deliver.

Your Key Responsibilities Include


Driving HTA and cross-trial analytics at launch speed. From analytics packages for large-scale multi-market submissions across AMNOG, NICE, HAS and EU HTA joint clinical assessments, to direct collaboration with affiliates on high-profile evidence synthesis pieces that shape payer decisions in individual markets.
Building analytical infrastructure and scalable workflows. Develop reproducible pipelines, automation tools and data models that enable the team to handle portfolio growth while maintaining quality.
Partnering across functions and markets. Work with biostatisticians, regulatory data scientists, health economists, and affiliate organisations worldwide, with an enterprise mindset and a focus on building ways of working that scale beyond individual projects.
Pioneering AI-augmented workflows. Design and implement processes where agents handle throughput and statisticians focus on what requires genuine scientific judgment, reimagining the HTA data scientist role from the ground up with a sharp eye for where AI creates real value.


Your new department

Cross-Trial Evidence & HTA (CTEHTA) is a specialist statistics and data science function of approximately 20 statisticians and data scientists, delivering evidence generation from early clinical development through launch and lifecycle management across the full Novo Nordisk portfolio.

We produce work that is scrutinised by HTA bodies, payers, and the scientific community, which means transparency, auditability and methodological credibility are baseline requirements. When we get it right, the outcome is straightforward: the right medicines reach the right patients at the right time.

We are a small team where everyone's contribution is visible, which means high expectations but also high trust. You will have direct visibility into leadership decisions, your technical judgment will shape project direction, and the gap between having an idea and implementing it is short.

Your Skills And Qualifications


MSc, preferably PhD, in statistics, health economics, mathematics, computer science, or a related quantitative field
Several years of relevant industry experience with demonstrated impact on evidence generation for drug development or market access
Strong experience of direct relevance to HTA statistics and evidence synthesis (indirect treatment comparisons, network meta-analysis, real-world evidence, health economic modelling)
Strong programming skills in R or Python, including reproducible pipelines, automation tools, and heterogeneous data sources
Familiarity with the evidence-to-access pathway, including how analytical choices affect reimbursement outcomes across major HTA frameworks
Strong project management skills, with the ability to manage multiple complex workstreams in parallel and bring the same rigour to project execution as to analytical work
A concrete perspective on AI in regulated environments, grounded in practical engagement with how AI tools and automated workflows apply to statistical or scientific work


Working at Novo Nordisk

We're not chasing quick fixes – we're creating lasting change for long-term health. For over 100 years, we've been driven by a single purpose: to defeat serious chronic diseases and help millions of people live healthier lives. This dedication fuels our constant curiosity and inspires us to push the boundaries of what's possible in healthcare. We embrace diverse perspectives, seek out bold ideas, and build partnerships rooted in shared purpose. Together, we're making healthcare more accessible, treating and preventing diseases, and pioneering solutions that create change spanning generations. When you join us, you become part of something bigger – a legacy of impact that reaches far beyond today. The video below features colleagues worldwide speaking about why they’re proud to work at Novo Nordisk and highlights the culture, collaboration and long-term impact. Together, for the long-term!

What We Offer

At Novo Nordisk, you’ll join a global healthcare company with a unique culture and strong results. We offer continuous learning, career development, and benefits tailored to your life and career stage.

Salary: For this role, the Annual Base Salary ranges from 742,600.00 to 1,091,600.00 DKK, corresponding to the level of the position.

The placement within the salary range will be assessed during the recruitment process based on the candidate’s skills, competencies, knowledge, and relevant experience.

Incentives and Benefits: The salary package may include short-term and/or long-term incentives as well as other employee benefits based on position level, location, functional area and relevant market benchmarks.

Learn more about our Reward Philosophy here.

Deadline

Apply before 26 May 2026.

Please include a few sentences about why you are applying for this position in your resume or CV. You do not need to attach a cover letter to your application. To ensure an efficient and fair recruitment process, please refrain from adding a photo to your CV.

We commit to an inclusive recruitment process and equality of opportunity for all our job applicants

 

At Novo Nordisk we recognize that it is no longer good enough to aspire to be the best company in the world. We need to aspire to be the best company for the world and we know that this is only possible with talented employees with diverse perspectives, backgrounds and cultures. We are therefore committed to creating an inclusive culture that celebrates the diversity of our employees, the patients we serve and communities we operate in. Together, we’re life changing.

Title: Open Talent Pool - all DHI locations
Place: Hørsholm, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
At DHI, water, environment and sustainability meet innovation. Join us in enabling a sustainable future for water!

Looking to make an impact on our planet? At DHI, you can. We work with water in all its forms – combining deep domain knowledge, advanced digital technology, and more than 60 years of research and innovation to help society use, manage and live with water sustainably. With over 1,100 engineers and specialists across 26 countries, we are continually expanding our global community of experts.

As we grow, new opportunities emerge throughout the year. Even when a specific vacancy isn’t yet published, we welcome proactive applications from individuals who want to contribute to our mission – including senior specialist with years of experience or students, recent graduates and early career professionals who are excited to build their future in the water sector.

Why submit an open application?

Our business evolves quickly, and new roles – technical, scientific, digital and business-oriented – are created as projects grow and priorities shift. Submitting an open application adds you to our Open Talent Pool, enabling us to reach out when a suitable opportunity arises, often before it is advertised.

Before you submit your open application, please make sure to check our current vacancies as well.

Opportunities for Students & Early Career Talent

We strongly encourage students and early career professionals to apply.

At DHI, You Can


Join as an intern, student assistant, thesis collaboration partner or industry master program student
Contribute to global water projects alongside experienced specialists
Apply your academic knowledge to real-world environmental and digital challenges
Grow your skills in engineering, modelling, climate resilience, data science, software development, sustainability and more
Work in a culture that supports experimentation, learning and exploration


If you are studying or recently graduated in engineering, environmental science, climate studies, digital technology, or related fields – and want to help shape a sustainable future – your application is very welcome.

Profiles we are interested in

We invite applications from experienced professionals, graduates, and students with interest or expertise in:


Engineering & Environmental Science Coastal, marine, environmental, hydraulic, climate, civil, ecosystem modelling
Data, Digital & Technology Software development, data science, machine learning, AI, digital product development
Consulting & Project Delivery Advisory services, modelling, client engagement, project/programme management
Sustainability & ESG
Corporate Functions Legal, compliance, HR, finance, operations, commercial roles


We value cross disciplinary thinkers—if your background doesn’t fit into a category, we still encourage you to apply.

What We Offer

When you join DHI, you are empowered to be an explorer—to innovate, collaborate, and continuously learn. You’ll be part of a global organisation committed to protecting water ecosystems and developing sustainable solutions for communities, cities, industries and the planet.

Positions May Be


Fulltime or parttime
Based in various global locations or open to flexible arrangements
Project based or permanent, depending on organisational needs


How To Apply


Please submit your application through our careers page under “Open Talent”. If you are interested in other locations, please indicate that clearly.


Kindly Attach


Your CV
A brief note outlining your area of interests, skills and how you found DHI
List your preferred DHI location(s)
Links to portfolio, publications, or project work (optional)


Learn more about DHI www.dhigroup.com
Show more

Title: Lead Engineer, Data Platform
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Join Pandora to help lead one of the most compelling data engineering challenges in 2026: the Data & AI Platform that sits underneath the world's largest jewellery company. The platform serves teams across consumer engagement, retail supply chain, finance, merchandising, and manufacturing, operating across more than 100 countries and 6,800 points of sale. What it enables sets the ceiling on what the rest of the business can do with data.

As Lead Engineer for the Data Platform, you are the most senior IC on the team. You set the technical direction for the platform alongside the Engineering Manager, unblock the engineers around you, and personally lead the highest-leverage, highest-risk initiatives end to end — from problem framing through rollout and adoption.

Key Responsibilities


Set the technical direction for the Data & AI Platform; own the architecture across Databricks, the Microsoft Fabric and Power BI platform layer.
Lead the highest-leverage, highest-risk initiatives end to end — from problem framing through delivery, rollout, and adoption across product teams.
Raise the engineering bar across the team through design reviews, code reviews, incident reviews, and patterns that scale beyond a single service.
Partner with the Engineering Manager on roadmap, staffing, and the technical trade-offs behind them.
Represent the platform in Pandora-wide architecture and governance forums; influence peer teams so the platform is adopted, not imposed.
Mentor senior and mid-level engineers; multiply the impact of the people around you rather than bottlenecking on yourself.
Drive the patterns for batch, streaming, and ML/AI workloads, and for data contracts and shared schemas that make interoperability across domains the default.


What You Bring


Track record leading the technical direction of a production data or developer platform at scale, with measurable adoption across many product teams.
Deep expertise in distributed systems design, polyglot engineering, and infrastructure-as-code at scale. Strong coding skills in Python; comfort in at least one additional language.
Hands-on experience with Databricks, Delta Lake, and Spark (or equivalent lakehouse technologies), and working knowledge of the Microsoft data ecosystem — Fabric, Power BI, Azure.
Deep Terraform and CI/CD experience, including modules, workspaces, and policy-as-code (OPA, Sentinel, or equivalent) across multi-account patterns.
Demonstrated ability to drive adoption of platform capabilities.


Nice To Have


Experience building internal developer platforms, platform SDKs, or golden paths that product teams consume at scale.
Familiarity with FinOps practices — cost modelling, chargeback and showback, optimising compute, storage, and egress at scale.
Background in retail, consumer products, manufacturing, or another multi-region, multi-domain enterprise.


Did we get your attention?

We hope you got all your questions answered and feel confident applying for this position. If you see yourself in the role and would like to become a part of Pandora’s future, please do not hesitate to apply! We are an international team, so please make sure to send us your application in English to be considered. We look forward to hearing from you!

We process applications on a continuous basis, which is why we encourage you to send your application as soon as possible. You can also read more about Pandora on our corporate site www.pandoragroup.com.

Icing On The Cake

You will be working out of our beautiful Digital & Technology Hub in Copenhagen, in a rapidly growing environment of highly skilled and experienced colleagues, which is a key initiative that goes along with Pandora’s experience & ambitions.Besides our beautiful interior design, we are also proud of our LEED Gold Certification for Energy and Environmental Design. This means that we use as few natural resources as possible and that we use them with respect. The result is a professional and dynamic space that is unique, comfortable and inspirational – and delivers a true Pandora experience to anyone who works here or comes to visit.

About Pandora

Pandora is the world's largest jewellery brand, specialising in the design, crafting and marketing of accessible luxury jewellery made from high-quality materials. Each piece is created to inspire self-expression, allowing people to share their stories and passions through meaningful jewellery. Pandora jewellery is sold in more than 100 countries through 7,000 points of sale, including more than 2,800 concept stores.

Headquartered in Copenhagen, Denmark, Pandora employs around 39,000 people worldwide and crafts its jewellery with 100% recycled silver and gold. Pandora is committed to leadership in sustainability and has set out to halve greenhouse gas emissions across its value chain by 2030. Pandora is listed on the Nasdaq Copenhagen stock exchange and generated revenue of DKK 32.5 billion (EUR 4.4 billion) in 2025.

Title: Research Assistant in AI-Powered Educational Platform
Description:
Want to shape the future of university teaching with artificial intelligence? At the Department of Materials and Production, Aalborg University, we are looking for a Research Assistant to design and build an AI-powered education platform, from concept to classroom deployment. You will work hands-on with large language models, develop a full-stack prototype, and evaluate its impact through a real controlled study with students, all within a small, collaborative project team with direct access to the principal investigator.



The position is temporary for 12 months, at 37 hours per week (full-time), with a desired starting date of 1st July 2026.



  



Your work tasks



You will join a funded education project that designs, builds, and evaluates an AI-powered platform for education. The platform will be piloted in a real university course and assessed through a controlled study, with the goal of producing a working open-source prototype. Specific tasks include:



Software development:

Design and implement the AI for education platform, from front-end interface to back-end services and database.
Integrate large language model APIs and related AI services to power the teaching and tutoring components.
Build infrastructure for logging student interactions in a privacy-compliant manner (GDPR).
Deploy and maintain the system on university infrastructure.

 



Your competencies   



Required:

Strong programming skills in at least one modern web or backend language; ability to work across the full stack.
Practical experience building and deploying applications, including working with APIs and databases.
Hands-on experience with large language models or other generative AI tools in a development context.
Experience with quantitative or mixed-methods data analysis.

Preferred:

Background in educational technology, human-computer interaction, or learning analytics.
Familiarity with multi-agent AI systems or conversational AI development.
Experience with retrieval-augmented generation or similar context-aware AI techniques.
Experience as a teaching assistant or in an educational setting.

 



Qualification requirements 



Appointment as a Research Assistant requires academic qualifications at master's degree level in Computer Science, Software Engineering, Artificial Intelligence, Human-Computer Interaction, Educational Technology, or a closely related discipline.



The position is open to all nationalities. Applicants must hold a valid right to work in Denmark at the time of appointment.



 



Who we are    



The Department of Materials and Production has an active international research environment with projects spanning robotics, IoT, machine learning, and manufacturing systems. Research is conducted in close collaboration with industry partners and within AAU's distinctive Problem-Based Learning model.



This position is embedded in a small, focused project team. You will have direct access to the project PI, regular feedback.



You can read more about the department at www.mp.aau.dk



How to apply  



Your application must include the following: 

Application, stating reasons for applying, qualifications in relation to the position, and intentions and visions for the position. 
Curriculum Vitae (CV). 
Diplomas (master's degree diploma and potential PhD diploma). 
Additional qualifications in relation to the position. 
References/recommendations. 

You can read more about the requirements for your application here.    



The application must be submitted via Aalborg University’s recruitment system, which can be accessed under the job advertisement on Aalborg University's website.  



Aalborg University wants to reflect the surrounding society and has diversity as a core value. Therefore, everyone, regardless of personal background and orientation, is encouraged to apply for the position. 



 



Do you have any questions? 



If you have any questions about the position, you are more than welcome to contact us. You will find contact persons at the bottom of the jobpost.     



Interviews are expected to be held around second week of June.   



 



Further information 



Read more about our recruitment process here   



The appointment process at Aalborg University involves a shortlisting process. You can read more about the shortlisting and appointment process here.  



The hiring process at Aalborg University may include a risk assessment as a tool to identify potential risks associated with new hires, ensuring the safety, compliance, and integrity of the workplace. 



 



Salary and terms of employment  



The employment is in accordance with the Ministerial Order on the Appointment of Academic Staff at Universities (the Appointment Order) and the Ministerial Order on Job Structure for Academic Staff at Universities (in Danish) and protocol on certain terms of employment of academic staff at universities (in Danish). 



Salary and terms of employment are in accordance with the collective agreement between the Danish Confederation of Professional Associations and the state (AC collective agreement) (only in Danish) and protocol on certain terms of employment of academic staff at universities (only in Danish). 



There is a mutual probationary period of 3 months for the position.



Aalborg University - Knowledge for the world



Aalborg University is an international workplace with more than 3,700 employees. We offer real-world-oriented education and create world-class research results through collaboration between researchers, students, and public and private companies. HTML:
Want to shape the future of university teaching with artificial intelligence? At the Department of Materials and Production, Aalborg University, we are looking for a Research Assistant to design and build an AI-powered education platform, from concept to classroom deployment. You will work hands-on with large language models, develop a full-stack prototype, and evaluate its impact through a real controlled study with students, all within a small, collaborative project team with direct access to the principal investigator.

The position is temporary for 12 months, at 37 hours per week (full-time), with a desired starting date of 1st July 2026.

  

Your work tasks

You will join a funded education project that designs, builds, and evaluates an AI-powered platform for education. The platform will be piloted in a real university course and assessed through a controlled study, with the goal of producing a working open-source prototype. Specific tasks include:

Software development:

  • Design and implement the AI for education platform, from front-end interface to back-end services and database.
  • Integrate large language model APIs and related AI services to power the teaching and tutoring components.
  • Build infrastructure for logging student interactions in a privacy-compliant manner (GDPR).
  • Deploy and maintain the system on university infrastructure.

 

Your competencies   

Required:

  • Strong programming skills in at least one modern web or backend language; ability to work across the full stack.
  • Practical experience building and deploying applications, including working with APIs and databases.
  • Hands-on experience with large language models or other generative AI tools in a development context.
  • Experience with quantitative or mixed-methods data analysis.

Preferred:

  • Background in educational technology, human-computer interaction, or learning analytics.
  • Familiarity with multi-agent AI systems or conversational AI development.
  • Experience with retrieval-augmented generation or similar context-aware AI techniques.
  • Experience as a teaching assistant or in an educational setting.

 

Qualification requirements 

Appointment as a Research Assistant requires academic qualifications at master's degree level in Computer Science, Software Engineering, Artificial Intelligence, Human-Computer Interaction, Educational Technology, or a closely related discipline.

The position is open to all nationalities. Applicants must hold a valid right to work in Denmark at the time of appointment.

 

Who we are    

The Department of Materials and Production has an active international research environment with projects spanning robotics, IoT, machine learning, and manufacturing systems. Research is conducted in close collaboration with industry partners and within AAU's distinctive Problem-Based Learning model.

This position is embedded in a small, focused project team. You will have direct access to the project PI, regular feedback.

You can read more about the department at www.mp.aau.dk

How to apply  

Your application must include the following: 

  • Application, stating reasons for applying, qualifications in relation to the position, and intentions and visions for the position. 
  • Curriculum Vitae (CV). 
  • Diplomas (master's degree diploma and potential PhD diploma). 
  • Additional qualifications in relation to the position. 
  • References/recommendations. 

You can read more about the requirements for your application here.    

The application must be submitted via Aalborg University’s recruitment system, which can be accessed under the job advertisement on Aalborg University's website.  

Aalborg University wants to reflect the surrounding society and has diversity as a core value. Therefore, everyone, regardless of personal background and orientation, is encouraged to apply for the position. 

 

Do you have any questions? 

If you have any questions about the position, you are more than welcome to contact us. You will find contact persons at the bottom of the jobpost.     

Interviews are expected to be held around second week of June.   

 

Further information 

Read more about our recruitment process here   

The appointment process at Aalborg University involves a shortlisting process. You can read more about the shortlisting and appointment process here.  

The hiring process at Aalborg University may include a risk assessment as a tool to identify potential risks associated with new hires, ensuring the safety, compliance, and integrity of the workplace. 

 

Salary and terms of employment  

The employment is in accordance with the Ministerial Order on the Appointment of Academic Staff at Universities (the Appointment Order) and the Ministerial Order on Job Structure for Academic Staff at Universities (in Danish) and protocol on certain terms of employment of academic staff at universities (in Danish). 

Salary and terms of employment are in accordance with the collective agreement between the Danish Confederation of Professional Associations and the state (AC collective agreement) (only in Danish) and protocol on certain terms of employment of academic staff at universities (only in Danish). 

There is a mutual probationary period of 3 months for the position.

Aalborg University - Knowledge for the world

Aalborg University is an international workplace with more than 3,700 employees. We offer real-world-oriented education and create world-class research results through collaboration between researchers, students, and public and private companies. This is how we achieve insights, new solutions to societal problems, and knowledge that changes the world. Our main campus is in Aalborg, but we also have campuses in Esbjerg and Copenhagen.

Title: Research Engineer, Robotics Foundation Models
Description:
Research Engineer, Robotics Foundation Models at Qualia




Qualia is the company that brings foundation models into the real world: data collection, fine-tuning, inference, and on-device deployment, end to end. We're a small, technical team in Copenhagen and Stockholm building this for manufacturers, in partnership with top companies. Qualia has raised roughly USD 10M to pursue the hardest problems in robotics.




We're hiring a research engineer to push the model side of our research. VLAs, WAMs, that work on real customer data. RL that closes the gap on precision tasks. World models, UMI, EgoData, that let us iterate faster than collecting another 1,000 episodes of teleop.




This is not a research role at a lab. This is a research engineer role at a startup. Your work ships to customers, runs on robots, and gets judged by whether the task succeeds.




What you'll work on

VLA fine-tuning at the edge of what's possible
VLAs on customer and internal data. Make them work where the base model fails.
Build the recipe. Subtask decomposition, stage classifiers, relative actions, action chunking, normalization. Everything that turns "we got 30% success" into "we got 99% success."
Push the data pipeline upstream so that fine-tuning is repeatable across robots and tasks, not a one-off.




RL on top of VLAs

Residual policies for high-precision sub-movements (key insertion, peg-in-hole, force-sensitive contact).
RL-driven training loops on top of VLAs and WAMs.
Sim-to-real where it accelerates a real customer task, not for its own sake.




World models and synthetic data

Build the workflow that turns a small amount of real teleop into a much larger training corpus.
Decide what's worth using in our platform and what isn't.
You'll own model side end to end: data, training, inference, evaluation, iteration.




Who we're looking for:

Required

Strong research track record OR strong applied robotics ML track record. Either path works.
Hands-on with at least one VLA stack. Trained one, evaluated one, debugged one. Not just read the paper.
Comfortable with the main ML frameworks. Comfortable on Linux. Comfortable on a real robot (or eager to be).
Comfortable with messy data and the realization that 80% of the work is making the data not lie to you.
Comfortable shipping. Models in production beat models in arxiv at this company.




Bonus

Real teleoperation or data collection experience
RL beyond toy environments
Experience with sim-to-real, world models, or video-conditioned generation
Familiarity with VLAs
You've shipped a robot policy that a non-engineer used and didn't break




What we don't care about

Whether you have a PhD or you don't
Whether you've worked at a famous lab or you haven't
Whether your background is industry, academia, or self-taught




We care that you've done the thing.




How we work

Small team, fast loop. Around 10 people. Repos move fast. No PR purgatory.
Real customers from day one. You'll talk to pilots directly.
Strong opinions, loosely held. We argue, we ship, we update. You'll be expected to push back when we're wrong.
Hardware is real. Open Arm, R1 Light, Flexiv's, Thors. Not just sim.




What we offer

Competitive salary + meaningful equity (early-stage)
Working on robotics foundation models with a team that ships
Direct line to founders and customers
Copenhagen office, Stockholm satellite opening end of summer
Real budget for compute, hardware, and other things that matter
The usual good stuff




Apply here and or email fabian@qualiastudios.dev with whatever best represents your work and/or a resume.

Title: Software developer with AI development focus
Place: Brøndby Municipality, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Milestone Systems is a leading provider of data-driven video technology software. Our portfolio includes XProtect video management software, Arcules video surveillance as a service, and BriefCam analytics. We amplify what organizations of any size can see, do, and achieve with video. Guided by our People First mindset - freedom, inclusion, and togetherness - we always consider our employees, partners, and communities in everything we do.

For our Digital Presence department, we are looking for a talented and forward-thinking Software Developer who is passionate about building modern, scalable applications using C#, JavaScript, and Microsoft Azure. 
You will play a key role in developing and enhancing our digital solutions—while also leveraging AI tools and techniques to improve software development processes and product capabilities.

Global IT at Milestone Systems delivers IT solutions that empower our business, our channel partners, and our customers worldwide. We safeguard and continuously evolve our enterprise IT architecture, define robust solution architectures, and lead the way in IT security by establishing clear policies, behavioral guidelines, and compliance standards.

Our organization provides a broad portfolio of services, including software development, cloud services, cybersecurity, data analytics, digital workplace solutions, and end-user support. We are committed to delivering high-quality, reliable solutions that support a rapidly evolving digital landscape.

As part of Global IT, you will work closely with business stakeholders, partners, and customers to deliver solutions that directly support Milestone’s commercial processes and make a real impact on how we operate globally.

 

Why this role matters
In this position, you will contribute to the development and modernization of applications and services that deliver complex functionality to both internal and external users. These solutions include hardware configuration calculators, training environment provisioning tools, and product download management systems.

Currently, many of these services run on IIS-based virtual servers. Your mission will be to help transform them into scalable, secure, cloud-native solutions built on modern Azure serverless architecture.

This role requires proficiency across the full stack, with a clear emphasis on backend technologies, combined with experience in React and an understanding of modern cloud-based application patterns.
 

What you’ll be doing


Design, develop, and maintain applications using C# (.NET) and JavaScript frameworks
Stay up to date on emerging technologies, especially in AI-assisted development
Build and deploy cloud-native solutions using Microsoft Azure
Integrate AI tools and services (e.g., Azure OpenAI, Claude Code, automation tools, coding assistants) into development workflows
Write clean, maintainable, and testable code following best practices
Optimize application performance, scalability, and security 
 


What we are looking for


Experience with C# / .NET
Experience with JavaScript (Preferably React, Angular, or Vue)
Hands-on experience with Microsoft Azure (App Services, Functions, APIs, DevOps, etc.)
Experience using AI tools in development, such as: 
Code generation tools (e.g., GitHub Copilot, Claude Code)
AI APIs (e.g., OpenAI / Azure AI services)
Understanding of REST APIs, microservices, and cloud architecture
Familiarity with version control (Git)
Great sense of humor 
 


Nice to Have


Experience with CI/CD pipelines (Azure DevOps, GitHub Actions)
Experience with Scrum and agile frameworks
 


Education
bachelor’s degree in computer science, Engineering, or a related field.
 

Experience
1–3 years of experience
 

Soft Skills
Strong problem-solving abilities, attention to detail, clear communication skills, and a collaborative mindset.
 

 What you’ll get

Joining Milestone means becoming part of a passionate, forward-thinking team dedicated to driving digital transformation. We foster an environment that encourages creativity, collaboration, and technical excellence.
 

What We Offer


Opportunity to work with modern technologies and AI-driven development
Flexible working environment (remote/hybrid)
Competitive salary and benefits according to experience
Continuous learning and development opportunities
A collaborative and innovative team culture
 


Apply

We offer an inclusive and unbiased recruitment process where employment decisions are based solely on qualifications, regardless of race, color, ethnicity, gender identity, age, sexual orientation, or religion.

We kindly ask you do not to attach a cover letter to your application. Instead, please include a brief motivation for applying in your CV. We also recommend that you do not include a photo.

If this opportunity interests you, we encourage you to apply as soon as possible.

For further information, please contact Senior Manager Christian Keinicke Jensen at +45 88 300 300.

You can learn more about Milestone Systems via Facebook, LinkedIn, or Twitter, or by visiting www.milestonesys.com.

We review applications on an ongoing basis, and the position will remain open until a suitable candidate has been identified.

Please note that the position is on-site at our headquarters in Brøndby, Denmark, with the possibility of flexible working arrangements, and requires a valid Danish work visa.

Title: Scientific Software Developer
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Scientific Software Developer

Join the Carlsberg Research Laboratory (CRL) in Copenhagen and help us build the software that accelerates scientific discovery. We are rethinking how research is done by embedding software and AI directly into real-world scientific workflows across domains such as chemistry, bioinformatics, sensory science, and machine learning.

What you’ll be doing

You will design and build the systems that power modern research, from data pipelines to AI-enabled tools. You won’t just prototype ideas. You’ll turn them into reliable, production-grade software used by scientists every day.


Build and own end-to-end scientific software systems
Develop automation that removes manual research workflows
Integrate modern AI capabilities into real-world applications
Work closely with domain experts across sensory science, chemistry, and bioinformatics to translate research needs into scalable software
Translate complex scientific problems into clean, scalable technical solutions
Ship quickly, iterate, and continuously improve


What we’re looking for


Master’s degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a related technical field, or equivalent experience; a PhD is a plus
Strong foundation in software engineering and system design
Experience building scalable systems, APIs, and data pipelines
Familiarity with AI/ML systems and modern AI tools and SDKs


You:


You have experience building systems from scratch
Are comfortable working at the intersection of software and real-world experimentation
Communicate effectively with both engineers and domain experts
Are excited to learn new scientific domains
You are comfortable working in evolving problem spaces


What you can expect

You will join a multidisciplinary team working at the intersection of software, AI, and science. You will build systems that reduce manual work, improve reproducibility, and accelerate how quickly research turns into real-world impact. You will have the opportunity to collaborate with domain specialists in the world of bioinformatics, sensory science, chemistry, and machine learning.

You can expect a fixed-term contract for three years.

Interested?

Apply through the link. Please notice that this is the only way we accept applications.

To help ensure a smooth recruitment process, we kindly ask that all application documents be submitted in English.

You do not need to attach a cover letter to your application, but please include a few sentences about why you are applying in your resume or CV.

The deadline for the application is the 26th of May. We read applications continuously, and vacancies may be filled sooner than the deadline, so we encourage you to apply as early as possible.

We look forward to receiving your application.

Carlsberg Group: Brewing for a better today and tomorrow

For us success has always been in the diverse mix of our people, our beers and our brands. At Carlsberg, we want to recruit and develop people with a global mindset, cultural understanding, and international experience to ensure that our organization stays agile, inclusive and prepared for future growth. Only by acknowledging and harvesting from different perspectives and experiences, will we gain competitive advantage and leverage the effect of diversity for business growth. Carlsberg aims to create equal access to opportunity regardless of social identity, and we encourage everyone to apply regardless of gender, nationality, race, religion or any other characteristics protected by law.

Title: Senior Machine Learning Engineer
Description:
Senior Machine Learning Engineer – fra model til produktion




Er du passioneret omkring machine learning, MLOps og reel impact – ikke kun i Jupyter Notebooks? Hos nemlig arbejder vi målrettet med at bruge ML til at forbedre alt fra personalisering og kundeoplevelse til forecasting og operationel effektivitet – og gøre det stabilt i produktion




Vi har en veldrevet, GCP-baseret dataplatform med stærk governance og stabile pipelines. Nu er næste skridt at få ML til at leve i stor skala på tværs af forretningen. Derfor søger vi en Senior Machine Learning Engineer, der kan drive ML-projekter hele vejen fra idé til produktion – og sikre, at de virker i praksis.




Din rolle – fra data til drift i stor skala

Du bliver en del af DataMind – vores centrale data- og ML-team – hvor du samarbejder tæt med både data engineers, analytics-specialister og machine learning engineers. Sammen arbejder vi tværfagligt med både kommercielle og operationelle teams og dækker hele ML-livscyklussen: fra datasæt og feature engineering til modeludvikling, deployment, drift og løbende optimering.




Vi bygger en moderne ML-platform oven på en stærk dataplatform – med fokus på hurtig eksperimentering og stabil drift i produktion. Du får stor indflydelse på både tekniske valg og måden, vi arbejder med ML på.




Dit mandat

Du kommer ikke ind for at vedligeholde noget eksisterende – du kommer ind for at være med til at definere, hvordan ML skal bygges og drives hos nemlig. Du bliver en central drivkraft i at operationalisere ML i stor skala og får ansvar for at sætte retning for arkitektur, standarder og MLOps-praksis. Det betyder reel indflydelse på, hvordan vi bygger, deployer og driver ML-løsninger på tværs af forretningen.




Dine opgaver

Udvikle og træne robuste ML-modeller til kommercielle, operationelle og kundevendte use cases
Designe, bygge og vedligeholde skalerbare og testbare pipelines, feature flows og inferens-løsninger (API og batch)
Drive og videreudvikle vores MLOps-praksis: CI/CD, monitorering, versionering, datasporbarhed og automatiseret retraining
Sikre høj driftssikkerhed og lav manuel vedligeholdelse gennem automation og standardisering
Samarbejde tæt med data engineers om pipelines, datakontrakter og skalerbarhed
Sikre governance, monitorering og GDPR-compliance for alle produktionsmodeller
Være teknisk sparringspartner for forretningen og sikre, at ML-initiativer skaber målbar værdi




Sådan arbejder vi (tech & setup)

GCP-baseret dataplatform (bl.a. BigQuery og relaterede services)
Python som primært udviklingssprog
Fokus på CI/CD, testbarhed og reproducérbarhed
Tæt samarbejde mellem Data Engineering, ML og forretning
Fokus på at få modeller hele vejen i produktion – ikke kun prototyper




Din profil – vi leder efter dig, der…

Har solid erfaring med udvikling og drift af ML-løsninger i produktion
Er stærk i Python, feature engineering og modeludvikling i skala
Har hands-on erfaring med MLOps, herunder CI/CD, deployment og monitorering
Har erfaring med cloud – gerne GCP
Trives i et miljø med høj faglighed, ansvar og tæt samarbejde




Derfor skal du vælge nemlig

Vi er Danmarks største online supermarked – men i praksis et datadrevet tech scale-up med store ambitioner.




Hos os får du:

Reelt ejerskab og indflydelse på vores ML-retning og setup
Kort vej fra idé til produktion – vi prioriterer execution over slides
Mulighed for at arbejde med ML, der bliver brugt og skaber værdi hver dag

HTML:
Senior Machine Learning Engineer – fra model til produktion


Er du passioneret omkring machine learning, MLOps og reel impact – ikke kun i Jupyter Notebooks? Hos nemlig arbejder vi målrettet med at bruge ML til at forbedre alt fra personalisering og kundeoplevelse til forecasting og operationel effektivitet – og gøre det stabilt i produktion


Vi har en veldrevet, GCP-baseret dataplatform med stærk governance og stabile pipelines. Nu er næste skridt at få ML til at leve i stor skala på tværs af forretningen. Derfor søger vi en Senior Machine Learning Engineer, der kan drive ML-projekter hele vejen fra idé til produktion – og sikre, at de virker i praksis.


Din rolle – fra data til drift i stor skala

Du bliver en del af DataMind – vores centrale data- og ML-team – hvor du samarbejder tæt med både data engineers, analytics-specialister og machine learning engineers. Sammen arbejder vi tværfagligt med både kommercielle og operationelle teams og dækker hele ML-livscyklussen: fra datasæt og feature engineering til modeludvikling, deployment, drift og løbende optimering.


Vi bygger en moderne ML-platform oven på en stærk dataplatform – med fokus på hurtig eksperimentering og stabil drift i produktion. Du får stor indflydelse på både tekniske valg og måden, vi arbejder med ML på.


Dit mandat

Du kommer ikke ind for at vedligeholde noget eksisterende – du kommer ind for at være med til at definere, hvordan ML skal bygges og drives hos nemlig. Du bliver en central drivkraft i at operationalisere ML i stor skala og får ansvar for at sætte retning for arkitektur, standarder og MLOps-praksis. Det betyder reel indflydelse på, hvordan vi bygger, deployer og driver ML-løsninger på tværs af forretningen.


Dine opgaver

  • Udvikle og træne robuste ML-modeller til kommercielle, operationelle og kundevendte use cases
  • Designe, bygge og vedligeholde skalerbare og testbare pipelines, feature flows og inferens-løsninger (API og batch)
  • Drive og videreudvikle vores MLOps-praksis: CI/CD, monitorering, versionering, datasporbarhed og automatiseret retraining
  • Sikre høj driftssikkerhed og lav manuel vedligeholdelse gennem automation og standardisering
  • Samarbejde tæt med data engineers om pipelines, datakontrakter og skalerbarhed
  • Sikre governance, monitorering og GDPR-compliance for alle produktionsmodeller
  • Være teknisk sparringspartner for forretningen og sikre, at ML-initiativer skaber målbar værdi


Sådan arbejder vi (tech & setup)

  • GCP-baseret dataplatform (bl.a. BigQuery og relaterede services)
  • Python som primært udviklingssprog
  • Fokus på CI/CD, testbarhed og reproducérbarhed
  • Tæt samarbejde mellem Data Engineering, ML og forretning
  • Fokus på at få modeller hele vejen i produktion – ikke kun prototyper


Din profil – vi leder efter dig, der…

  • Har solid erfaring med udvikling og drift af ML-løsninger i produktion
  • Er stærk i Python, feature engineering og modeludvikling i skala
  • Har hands-on erfaring med MLOps, herunder CI/CD, deployment og monitorering
  • Har erfaring med cloud – gerne GCP
  • Trives i et miljø med høj faglighed, ansvar og tæt samarbejde


Derfor skal du vælge nemlig

Vi er Danmarks største online supermarked – men i praksis et datadrevet tech scale-up med store ambitioner.


Hos os får du:

  • Reelt ejerskab og indflydelse på vores ML-retning og setup
  • Kort vej fra idé til produktion – vi prioriterer execution over slides
  • Mulighed for at arbejde med ML, der bliver brugt og skaber værdi hver dag
  • En moderne data- og ML-platform med solide fundamenter
  • En kultur præget af faglighed, transparens og samarbejde
  • Fleksibilitet og mulighed for hjemmearbejde – vi tager work-life balance seriøst

Title: Senior Machine Learning Engineer
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Tech-first. Ego-free. User-obsessed.




Over one million Danes use Skatteguiden to understand and optimise their taxes. We automate tax tracking, surface deductions people didn't know they had, and give users the insight they need to make better financial decisions — all connected directly to the Danish tax authority.




We're an independent company of around 50 people based in Copenhagen, with a 4.7-star rating and a simple mission: make financial insight accessible to everyone, not just those who can afford an accountant.




We have an ambitious multi-year vision for where we take the product next. This role owns the ML systems that make it possible.







About the role




Skatteguiden sits on a rich proprietary dataset — years of tax and financial data across more than a million Danes. That data fuels the intelligence behind our product, and the ML systems built on top of it are what turn it into something users feel.




You'll architect and orchestrate those ML systems — how we run experiments, compare models, deploy them, and manage them cleanly in production. The systems themselves serve user-facing insights: think surfacing opportunities users would otherwise miss, and helping them understand the consequences of financial decisions before they make them.




We have models in production today, but the deployment, serving, and lifecycle architecture deserves a dedicated owner who's done this before.




You'll join the AI Products team as the senior ML profile, working closely with our existing Data Scientist, our AI Engineer, and our incoming Applied AI Engineer. The team is small and dynamic, and this role carries meaningful technical leadership for the ML systems across Skatteguiden.







What you'll do




- Architect and orchestrate our ML layer. Design how we run experiments, compare models, deploy them, and keep them healthy in production. Models, features, pipelines, and the platform that connects them.

- Be the data source expert for the AI team. Partner with the data and backend teams, get the right data in the right shape for ML, and make that pipeline reliable.

- Build systems that compound. Personalisation and pattern detection that become more useful as more users are onboarded.

- Own your systems in production. Training, evaluation, rollout, monitoring, retraining. End-to-end.

- Set the technical bar. As the senior ML profile, you help shape how the team thinks about modelling, evaluation, and production ML discipline.







Who we're looking for




You're a senior ML engineer who has shipped production ML at scale and wants to build something with genuine user impact. You care about models, but you care more about whether they ship, whether they work, and whether they actually change what a user sees.




If you're excited about the task of lifting MLOps to the next level — enabling both rapid experimentation and reliable deployment of our models and agentic systems — we'd like to hear from you.




We also want to be upfront: priorities will shift, and the first months will include some classic data engineering work alongside the ML systems work. If LLMs are the only thing you want to do, this isn't the role.







Must-have




- Senior hands-on experience shipping production ML systems. You've owned models end-to-end: features, training, evaluation, deployment, monitoring, iteration. We want someone who can sit across from us and say "I've seen this, this is how it scales."

- Strong MLOps. Backend serving patterns, model-serving optimisation, deployment architecture, lifecycle management. This is the gap we're filling.

- Experience or strong interest in agentic systems and LLM-specific deployment concerns.

- Data engineering chops. Comfortable designing data pipelines and infrastructure, and partnering with the data team.

- Strong skills in Python and modern ML tooling.

- Clear communication, particularly on architectural trade-offs. You can explain to engineers, product people, and leadership why a system is designed the way it is.

- Security, privacy, and compliance awareness. You work with GDPR-sensitive data thoughtfully and you understand what ISO 27001 implies for model development and deployment.

- Genuine enthusiasm for AI. You use AI tools daily in your own work and you're always looking for new ways to apply them.







Nice-to-have




- Experience with financial, tax, or PSD2 banking data.

- Experience evaluating the LLM vs. classical ML boundary in production.

- Familiarity with Azure.

- Danish language — useful but not required. The team works in English.







Why join us




- A moat to build. Our ML layer is the technical foundation of Skatteguiden's long-term advantage. You'll be architecting and owning it.

- Real influence. Small team, high autonomy, and the decisions you make will shape our AI direction for years.

- The product matters. Over one million Danes use Skatteguiden. Your work turns their collective data into something each one of them benefits from.

- A serious roadmap. Concrete features, a multi-year vision, and an executive team that backs it.

- Solid company. Independent, profitable, growing. No VC pressure, no bloated roadmap. Just a clear mission and a team that cares.

- A company that's serious about AI. We use AI across the business, and we're only going deeper. Being a small, focused team means we can move fast on this — no lengthy approval chains, no slow rollouts.

- Good people. We take the work seriously without taking ourselves too seriously. The culture is professional, informal, and genuinely collaborative.







Practical details




Location: Copenhagen. We're an office-first company, and it's a deliberate choice. We're moving fast, we have big ambitions, and we genuinely believe that being physically together is one of the things that makes that possible. Things move quicker when you can turn your chair and talk to someone.




We're a five-minute walk from Rådhuspladsen, stocked with everything from fruit to fizzy drinks to afternoon snacks, and lunch is at Claus Meyer's canteen just across the street — proper food, not an afterthought. If you're putting in a long day, dinner is covered too.




Start: As soon as possible.

Salary: Competitive and based on your experience.







Our lean hiring process




1. Screening call with our recruiter (30 min) ONLINE

2. Technical interview with the Head of AI Products and an AI engineer (120 min) ONSITE

3. Case / technical deep-dive with the AI Products team (60 min) ONSITE

4. Final conversation with the CTO and CEO (30 min) ONSITE

Title: Concrete Scientist
Description:
Concrete Scientist – Biomason | Denmark




Biomason is redefining how the world builds. Operating at the intersection of biotechnology and construction materials, the company has developed a breakthrough alternative to traditional cement using naturally occurring microorganisms. With a fully operational production facility in Denmark and commercial products already deployed across Europe and the US, Biomason is one of the few low carbon cement solutions proven at scale and ready for rapid expansion.

As the business scales its biocement and bioconcrete technologies into repeatable industrial production, it is strengthening its applied materials capability on site.




The Role




A highly practical, hands-on position focused on the development and optimisation of concrete and bioconcrete formulations within a live production environment.




Working closely with R&D and plant operations, you will:

Develop and test concrete mix designs, including aggregate blend optimisation
Conduct materials testing aligned to EN standards and support structured experimentation (DOE, data analysis)
Translate laboratory work into pilot and production scale applications
Work directly within plant environments, supporting mixing, pressing, and curing processes
Contribute to improving performance, durability, and consistency of products




Candidate Profile




We are seeking a Concrete Scientist / Engineer with strong applied experience across cementitious materials:

Experience in concrete, cementitious materials, or related environments
Hands-on experience with mix design and aggregate blending
Exposure to precast concrete manufacturing or similar production environments (highly desirable)
Familiarity with EN standards and materials testing methodologies
Experience within plant, production, or quality environments, with involvement in mix / recipe development
Comfortable operating across laboratory and industrial settings




You bring a practical, solution-oriented mindset, with the ability to work closely with materials, processes, and production teams to deliver real-world outcomes.




About Beaumont Bailey




Beaumont Bailey is an executive search firm specialising in industrial manufacturing, building materials, and decarbonisation-led technologies. We partner with innovative organisations to appoint technical and operational talent critical to scaling next-generation industrial solutions.

We are supporting Biomason on this appointment as part of its continued European expansion.

Title: AI Deployment Engineer
Description:
AI Deployment Engineer – Smart Manufacturing AI Systems

Hybrid (Copenhagen, Denmark)

AI | MLOps | Industrial AI | Smart Manufacturing




About the Role

We’re looking for an AI Deployment Engineer to support the rollout of AI-driven systems used in smart manufacturing and industrial automation environments.




You’ll work on deploying and maintaining machine learning systems that support:

predictive maintenance
production optimisation
quality inspection
and operational intelligence across connected industrial platforms.




This role is ideal for engineers who enjoy working at the intersection of:

AI systems
cloud infrastructure
DevOps
and large-scale operational environments.




What You’ll Be Working On

Production deployment of AI systems supporting industrial automation workflows
Infrastructure for real-time monitoring and predictive analytics
Scalable AI rollout across connected manufacturing environments
CI/CD pipelines for machine learning and operational AI systems




Key Responsibilities

Deploy and manage machine learning models in production environments
Build and maintain CI/CD pipelines for AI workflows
Containerise and orchestrate services using Docker and Kubernetes
Monitor model performance, reliability, and system health
Collaborate with AI engineers and platform teams to optimise deployment strategies
Support model lifecycle management, rollback strategies, and observability
Ensure scalability and resilience of AI infrastructure in operational environments




Required Skills & Experience

3+ years experience in DevOps, MLOps, or cloud infrastructure engineering
Strong experience with CI/CD pipelines and automation
Hands-on experience with Docker and containerised environments
Experience with cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, or GCP)
Familiarity with machine learning deployment workflows
Understanding of scalable distributed systems and infrastructure reliability




Nice to Have

Experience with Kubernetes or orchestration platforms
Exposure to industrial systems, IoT, or manufacturing environments
Familiarity with monitoring and observability tooling
Experience supporting real-time AI inference systems




Why Join?

Work on real-world AI systems driving industrial transformation
Hybrid flexibility in Copenhagen
High-impact role in a growing Industrial AI / Industry 4.0 environment
Opportunity to contribute to large-scale AI adoption in manufacturing




If you’re passionate about deploying scalable AI systems into real operational environments, we’d love to hear from you.




Apply via XpertDirect — connecting companies with advanced AI infrastructure and deployment specialists.

Title: Junior Unity Developer & XR tester (Part time student job)
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Are you interested in helping develop and test digital solutions that can make a difference and help save lives?




Come join the Strategic Research team at Laerdal Copenhagen!




THE JOB

As our new Junior Unity Developer and XR tester, you will become part of our Strategic Research team, focusing on XR Production.

The Laerdal Strategic Research (SR) team is a diverse and experienced group specializing in research, development, and international collaboration.




The SR team focuses on scaling lifesaving impact by supporting lifesavers through better training, equipment, and system. Through generating evidence and supporting evidenced solutions, the SR team directly supports Laerdal’s overarching mission of Helping Save Lives.




The SR team connects science and technology to real-world impact by maintaining close, ethical collaboration with global research partners and key opinion leaders through collaborative research and meaningful innovation that advance lifesaving solutions.




The SR team works in an iterative, exploratory way - building, testing, learning, and refining ideas together with internal and external partners.




In this position, you will become part of our office in Copenhagen, and work closely with colleagues, stakeholders, and partners across Laerdal. You should expect ongoing collaboration with the SR team in Stavanger, Norway, as well as some travel. This may include:

Demos and prototype evaluations
Partner and stakeholder meetings
Conferences and events
Occasional visits to Laerdal’s headquarter in Stavanger







Your day-to-day responsibilities will be:




Prototype Development

Assist in the design and development of prototypes using Unity under the supervision of senior team members.
Help set up XR scenarios, implement basic functionality, and prepare prototype builds.
Provide feedback and suggestions to improve prototypes and user experience.




Testing & Quality Assurance

Execute manual test procedures thoroughly and accurately report, track, and verify defects.
Reproduce, investigate, and re-test incidents reported by customers or partners.
Contribute to usability, accessibility, performance, and security evaluation of prototypes.




Hardware, Headsets & Build Management

Set up and maintain XR headsets and related hardware.
Upload, manage, and maintain prototype builds and management software.
Ensure devices and tools are updated and functioning correctly.




Collaboration & Processes

Participate in meetings and research processes.
Perform or request code/design reviews and seek guidance when needed.
Assist with tasks outside primary discipline when relevant.
Update documentation, confluence pages, and user manuals.
Communicate challenges proactively with team and stakeholders.




You are...

Passionate and curious about new technology.
Currently pursuing studies in Computer Science or a closely related discipline.
Familiar with testing approaches and concepts.
Proficient with C# or other object-oriented programming languages
Knowledgeable of game engines such as Unity
Detail oriented and structured
Proactive, self-motivated and have a can-do attitude.
You are motivated by Laerdal’s mission of helping save lives.




It would be desirable if you:

are familiar with Git (or other version control systems)
have basic understanding of UI and UX principles.
XR knowledge is a plus but not required.
3D software knowledge is a plus but not required




Your Availability

Please, note that your application will not be considered unless:

You have at least 3 semesters left on your longest planned education.
You can work at least 12 hours a week.




Interested?

Apply with your CV and a short motivation explaining why this role speaks to you. (Remember, LinkedIn allows only one document, so please merge your CV and motivation letter into one file.)




If you have any questions that might keep you from applying, please reach out by e-mail to recruiting manager Bo Søhøj on bo.sohoj@laerdal.com




We’ll review applications and set up interviews on an ongoing basis.




THE COMPANY

Laerdal Copenhagen is the center of excellence for Laerdal's digital learning solutions. We provide best-in-class digital learning products, helping doctors and nurses around the world save lives.

With a unique mix of talents - ranging from medical doctors and user-experience designers to software engineers and talents from the video game industry - we work with international partners to deliver cutting-edge digital learning solutions that help save lives. We offer a collaborative and dynamic workplace characterized by a can-do attitude, an appreciation of diversity, flexible working conditions, and a casual atmosphere in an old warehouse in the center of Copenhagen.

You can get a feel of who we are in this video: https://vimeo.com/917084721/73a5d9e58a
